title: Deep-Space LDPC Batch Decoder
strategies: do-nothing decoder (checker baseline): keep frames that are already codewords, zero the rest -> corrects only the clean frames
weight-1 syndrome decoding: precompute single-bit-error syndromes (columns of H) and flip one bit when the syndrome matches a unique column
Gallager hard-decision bit-flipping: iteratively flip the coordinate in the most unsatisfied parity checks until the syndrome vanishes -> catches multi-bit patterns

stronger decoders (belief-propagation / ordered-statistics / bounded error enumeration / information-set decoding) recover more heavy-error frames; nearest-codeword decoding is NP-hard so the true optimum is unreachable
